repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem
Can't open swapfile when using a device path starting with `//?/`, because `?` is a reserved char.

## Solution
For device UNC path, replace `//?/UNC/` `//./UNC/` with `//`. For other device paths, only trim the device path prefix (`//?/`, `//./`). This also lets them use the same swapfile naming scheme as standard DOS and UNC paths.

close #31606, close #29495

comment_data.body:
would it make sense to this to be part of `FileInfo`?

`FileInfo` is not a great abstraction but ... it's confusing to add another one...
instead  of a new function could this be a flag on `os_fileinfo`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1809,6 +1809,36 @@ int path_is_url(const char *p)
   return 0;
 }
 
+/// Identify Windows path type and provide root offset.
+/// TODO(ntdiary): Could be extended for path.c cleanup and path normalization logic,
+/// such as root length calculation, relative path component evaluation.
+PathInfo path_parse_info(char *path)

comment_data.body:
`str:format()` is usually more readable. (same for other cases in this PR)

    eq(('%s%s.swp'):format(swapdir, dos_path:gsub('[:/]', '%%')), fn.swapname('%'))
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -118,6 +118,41 @@ describe('startup', function()
     command('filetype detect')
     eq('filetype detection:ON  plugin:OFF  indent:OFF', exec_capture('filetype'))
   end)
+
+  it('opens swap file correctly when using device path #31606', function()
+    skip(not is_os('win'), 'N/A: Windows feature')
+    local cwd = vim.fs.normalize(vim.uv.cwd())
+    local swapdir = cwd .. '/Xtest_swap_dir/'
+    local drive, path = cwd:sub(1, 1), cwd:sub(3)
+    local dos_path = cwd .. '/nonexist/file'
+    local device_quest_path = '//?/' .. dos_path
+    local device_dot_path = '//./' .. dos_path
+    local device_unc_path = '//?/UNC/localhost/' .. drive .. '$' .. path
+    clear({
+      args = {
+        '--embed',
+        '--headless',
+        '--cmd',
+        'set directory=' .. swapdir .. '/',
+        '--',
+        device_quest_path,
+      },
+      merge = false,
+    })
+    eq(swapdir .. dos_path:gsub('[:/]', '%%') .. '.swp', fn.swapname('%'))
+    command('bw!')
+    api.nvim_buf_set_name(0, dos_path)
+    eq(swapdir .. dos_path:gsub('[:/]', '%%') .. '.swp', fn.swapname('%'))
+    command('bw!')
+    api.nvim_buf_set_name(0, device_dot_path)
+    eq(swapdir .. dos_path:gsub('[:/]', '%%') .. '.swp', fn.swapname('%'))

comment_data.body: I didn't notice there's also a `FileInfo`, I'll look into it later. :)

Eh, my comment in the code was a bit too brief and didn't really explain the intent.

My original idea was for it to hold information derived from the path string itself, and then pass it around during path parsing, similar to how `String` is passed around to avoid repeated `strlen` calls. it could eventually carry additional info, such as whether the path is a url, whether the path is absolute, or even a path pointer. From that perspective, I feel like it might make more sense as a separate type. Maybe it could use a better name?

BTW, I had considered whether it could help with the path issue in this [comment](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39844#issuecomment-4586649330), but I never envisioned it being associated with filesystem metadata like  `FileInfo`. :D

I plan to add some logic here that may not be suitable for `os_fileinfo`. That should allow us to walk the path only once and collect all the needed information, which would help reduce repeated helper calls like `path_is_url/path_with_url/path_is_absolute...`. After that, we could start cleaning up functions such as `fix_fname/vim_FullName/FullName_save...`

+/// such as root length calculation, relative path component evaluation.
+PathInfo path_parse_info(char *path)

comment_data.body: why isn't that suitable for `os_fileinfo` ? it does almost nothing currently, and presumably it makes sense to augment it. it could be an optional flag.

comment_data.body:
> From that perspective, I feel like it might make more sense as a separate type.

why doesn't that fit into `FileInfo` ? it sounds like the same thing, in fact the names are essentially synonyms (PathInfo vs FileInfo)

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1809,6 +1809,36 @@ int path_is_url(const char *p)
   return 0;
 }
 
+/// Identify Windows path type and provide root offset.
+/// TODO(ntdiary): Could be extended for path.c cleanup and path normalization logic,
+/// such as root length calculation, relative path component evaluation.
+PathInfo path_parse_info(char *path)

comment_data.body:
> The comment here ... makes me feel like wherever we only need string-level analysis, we should try to avoid calling real system path apis

Yes, that's the old "path" vs "filesystem" separation. But I'm skeptical that it will remain true for `path_parse_info`. Usually, the filesystem ends up needing to be consulted, so the whole thing falls apart, or results in an awkward interface where users need to start piecing things together.

The other factor at play is that `FileInfo` (and `FileID`... and `Directory`) are bad abstractions, and I don't want to add yet another one. We need to work on making these abstractions more meaningful rather than just serving up another one.

To unblock you, for now I suggest:

- introduce `os_fileinfo2` (this avoids needing to update all `os_fileinfo` callers)
- augment `FileInfo` instead of adding another new struct
- we can worry about the long-term interface later
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1809,6 +1809,36 @@ int path_is_url(const char *p)
   return 0;
 }
 
+/// Identify Windows path type and provide root offset.
+/// TODO(ntdiary): Could be extended for path.c cleanup and path normalization logic,
+/// such as root length calculation, relative path component evaluation.
+PathInfo path_parse_info(char *path)

comment_data.body: Ah, totally agree, I'll follow that style. As for short paths like `local swapdir = cwd .. '/Xtest_swap_dir'`, is `..` okay? Or always use `format`? :D

comment_data.body: for extremely simple cases, one `..` is fine, but I generally just avoid it because then people start copying it and over-using it

title: fix(path): open swapfile when using device path
